literature-report-plan.name: R2-ensemble-uncertainty

metadata.title: Ensemble Strategies and Uncertainty Quantification for Biomedical Segmentation

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Ensemble Strategies and Uncertainty Quantification for Biomedical Segmentation"
Focus: Deep ensembles vs model merging vs model soup for 3D segmentation. MC Dropout vs conformal prediction vs evidential deep learning. Calibration metrics (Brier score, ECE, reliability diagrams, IPA). How to select the champion model from a factorial design. Ensemble diversity vs accuracy tradeoff on small medical datasets (70 volumes, 3-fold CV).
Context: This report informs the NEUROVEX manuscript's evaluation pipeline and the champion selection logic in the deploy flow. The platform has 5 ensemble strategies implemented (per_loss_single_best, all_loss_single_best, per_loss_all_best, all_loss_all_best, cv_average). We need to know which strategies are scientifically justified and how to report them.
EXCLUDED topics: - SWA and post-training calibration details (report R1) - Agentic AI (report 5) - Multi-site/federated aspects (report R3) - Regulatory compliance (report R4)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: training, architecture Manuscript sections: Methods: Evaluation Pipeline (R2c), Results: Multi-Model Comparison (R3b)

seed-papers.paper: Lakshminarayanan et al. (2017). "Simple and Scalable Predictive Uncertainty Estimation using Deep Ensembles." NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/1612.01474
Fort et al. (2019). "Deep Ensembles: A Loss Landscape Perspective." arXiv. https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.02757
Jungo & Reyes (2019). "Assessing Reliability and Challenges of Uncertainty Estimations for Medical Image Segmentation." MICCAI. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-030-32245-8_6
Mehrtash et al. (2020). "Confidence Calibration and Predictive Uncertainty Estimation for Deep Medical Image Segmentation." IEEE TMI. https://doi.org/10.1109/TMI.2020.3006437
Maier-Hein et al. (2024). "Metrics Reloaded: Recommendations for image analysis validation." Nature Methods 21, 195-212. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-023-02151-z
Riley et al. (2021). "Stability of Random Forest Importance Measures." Briefings in Bioinformatics 22(4). https://doi.org/10.1093/bib/bbaa148
Ovadia et al. (2019). "Can You Trust Your Model's Uncertainty?" NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02530
Naeini et al. (2015). "Obtaining Well Calibrated Probabilities Using Bayesian Binning." AAAI. https://doi.org/10.1609/aaai.v29i1.9602
Rahaman & Thiery (2021). "Uncertainty Quantification and Deep Ensembles." NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.08792
Abe et al. (2022). "Deep Ensembles Work, But Are They Necessary?" NeurIPS. https://arxiv.org/abs/2202.06985

search-domains.domain: deep ensemble medical image segmentation uncertainty 2024 2025
model merging weight averaging segmentation diversity
conformal prediction volumetric segmentation coverage guarantee
calibration metrics Brier ECE medical imaging 2024
champion model selection factorial experiment segmentation
